aboutCondition: Early blight is a serious fungal disease that causes greasy brown patches on tomato leaves and can eventually kill the whole plant if not controlled.

recommendedActions.title: Prune Affected Leaves
recommendedActions.description: Carefully remove and destroy lower infected leaves to prevent spores from splashing upward.

recommendedActions.title: Modify Irrigation
recommendedActions.description: Avoid overhead watering. Apply water directly to the soil at the base of the plant to keep foliage dry.

recommendedActions.title: Apply Fungicide
recommendedActions.description: Use a copper-based fungicide or Bio-fungicide every 7-10 days if environmental conditions remain humid.
